topic: A wall calendar is missing the same date every month and those days cannot be remembered.
title: My Calendar Is Missing The Same Day Every Month | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: My wall calendar is missing the same date every single month.
scenes.image_prompt: a paper wall calendar in a dim kitchen, one date square blank and torn, low light, grain, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: It hangs in my kitchen, above the little table where I drink coffee.
scenes.image_prompt: a small kitchen table beneath a hanging calendar, a coffee mug, morning gloom through curtains, vertical 9:16 framing
a hand reaching toward a calendar page, soft window light, deep shadow at the edges, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The seventeenth was gone. No number, no line, just smooth paper.
scenes.image_prompt: a close framed calendar grid with one square blank and seamless, dim light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I checked my phone. The same day was blank there.
scenes.image_prompt: a phone showing a calendar app with one date faded out, held in a dark room, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: And I could not recall a single thing I had done that day.
scenes.image_prompt: a person standing at a window looking out, back to camera, foggy glass, dim interior, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: So I began writing notes to myself the night before.
scenes.image_prompt: a sticky note pad and pen on a nightstand, a lamp glowing, shadows, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Each note vanished by morning. Every month, the same date, erased.
scenes.image_prompt: an empty sticky note on a table where writing should be, soft morning light, grain, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: My neighbors swore that day never happened at all.
scenes.image_prompt: a quiet suburban street through a window, empty driveways, gray overcast, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Then I found one note that survived, in handwriting almost like mine.
scenes.image_prompt: a single sticky note with faint scrawled writing on a dark counter, harsh angled light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: It said I spend those days somewhere else, and I always agree to go.
scenes.image_prompt: an open door to a dark room, a coat and keys gone from the hook, dim hallway, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Tomorrow is the seventeenth again. I have started packing without knowing why.
scenes.image_prompt: a small packed bag by a front door at dusk, fog outside the window, deep shadow, vertical 9:16 framing
